BSC is only the Binance Alpha mirror. The v0.7.20+ pipeline auto-routes all on-chain-detection SQL (m6 / transfers / wash / holdings) to the {primary_chain} main-chain partition; all on-chain data in this report comes from the **{primary_chain}** main chain, and every wallet link in the report points to the {primary_chain} block explorer.

role_enum.insider_allocation_holder: Insider allocation holder (received a large lump near TGE from the project treasury chain)
role_enum.active_multi_token_allocator: Active multi-token allocator (received pre-launch / TGE allocations across multiple Alpha tokens)
role_enum.dex_mm_bot: DEX market-making bot (two-way trading against LP)
role_enum.wash_infra_member: Wash infrastructure member (one of the P / Q / X triplet — see Wash Infrastructure section)
role_enum.unknown_contract_small: Unknown small contract (< 5KB code, behavior inconclusive)

wash_infra.title: 🌀 Wash Infrastructure Detection (WASH INFRASTRUCTURE)
wash_infra.field_atomic_ratio: Atomic-pair tx ratio (X sends and receives in same tx_hash; 1.0 = router atomic swap signature)
wash_infra.field_p_drift: P net-flow drift (P cumulative in - out %; ≈ 0 = no inventory, wash's left pocket)
wash_infra.field_q_drift: Q net-flow drift (Q same metric, wash's right pocket)
wash_infra.field_diversity: tx_from diversity (= unique signers / total tx; < 0.05 = single operator)

class_enum.wash_infrastructure_routed: Routed wash — multi-user wash router (tx_from diversity > 0.3)
class_enum.wash_infrastructure_operator_controlled: Operator-controlled wash — single operator closed loop (tx_from diversity < 0.05)
class_enum.wash_infrastructure_ambiguous: Ambiguous wash — diversity in the middle band, classification pending

class_oneliner.wash_infrastructure_routed: 🎯 **Behaviour**: commercial wash router — shared infrastructure serving multiple upstream users (tx_from diversity > 0.3). Not a single project operator.
class_oneliner.wash_infrastructure_operator_controlled: 🎯 **Behaviour**: single-operator closed-loop wash — the executor X cycles tokens between P and Q to fabricate volume; no real buyer is involved. Differs from a genuine market maker (P/Q drift would be non-zero).
class_oneliner.wash_infrastructure_ambiguous: 🎯 **Behaviour**: ambiguous — diversity 0.05–0.3, operator-controlled vs routed cannot be cleanly assigned. Requires upstream/downstream signer analysis.

wash_infra.how_to_read_block:
> How to read this section (all three core metrics low = wash bot fingerprint):
>
> - **Atomic-pair tx ratio** = fraction of txs where the executor (X) **both sends and receives** the token in the same tx_hash. Real buyers/sellers only do one side per tx (< 30%); **a ratio near 1.0 means this wallet never makes one-sided trades — every tx is an atomic swap inside a router contract** (left buy + right sell in one call). This is the wash-infrastructure signature.
> - **P net-flow drift** = how far P's (the buy-side maker's) cumulative tokens-in differs from tokens-out, in percent. P is the wash's left pocket. **Near 0 = P never accumulates** (in 100, out 100, drift = 0). A real holder either accumulates (drift > 0) or distributes (drift < 0); they can never be exactly 0.
> - **Q net-flow drift** = same metric for Q (sell-side maker), the wash's right pocket. Near 0 means Q doesn't accumulate either.
>
> All three near-floor means X is shuttling tokens between P and Q to fake volume — no real buyer is involved. A genuine market maker has nonzero P/Q drift (they earn spread by holding inventory).
>
> tx_from diversity < 0.05 = the wash basically serves one upstream signer (operator-controlled); > 0.3 = shared by many users (commercial wash router).

flow_operators.title: 🤖 Cross-Alpha / single-operator flow wallets (FLOW OPERATORS)
flow_operators.how_to_read_block:
> How to read this section (3 core signals):
>
> - **tx_from diversity < 0.05** = the wallet's activity is driven by ≤ 2 upstream signers → single-operator control
> - **Cross-Alpha token count ≥ 5** = same wallet runs the same strategy across ≥5 Alpha tokens in 60 days → cross-Alpha bot
> - **Top-2 counterparty ratio ≥ 0.80** = ≥80% of the wallet's txs on this token go through just 2 addresses → async wash or arb pipeline
>
> These wallets carry **tiny net inventory but enormous flow** — they are the main source of fake surface volume. Real takers are far less than the headline 24h vol. Monitoring their entries / exits can lead-indicate similar moves on other Alpha tokens. Sub-classes: DEX_ARB_BOT (counterparties are routers/aggregators), ASYNC_WASH (counterparties are plain EOAs), CROSS_ALPHA_OPERATOR (cross-token activity).
flow_operators.col_n_alpha: Alpha tokens active in last 60 d
flow_operators.col_n_60d: Last-60d total tx count (across all tokens)
flow_operators.col_diversity: Upstream signer concentration (= unique tx_from / total tx; lower = more single-operator)
flow_operators.col_top2_ratio: Top-2 counterparty share of this-token tx %
flow_operators.cross_alpha_h4: Cross-Alpha activity ({n} tokens, by last-60d tx DESC; parens = tx count · chain)

subclass_oneliner.DEX_ARB_BOT: 🎯 **Behaviour**: cross-DEX / cross-venue arbitrage bot — buys the token in one pool and sells through another route in the same tx, capturing the price spread. Holds no inventory.
subclass_oneliner.ASYNC_WASH: 🎯 **Behaviour**: async P → X → Q wash — the same operator controls three wallets and shuttles the token across separate txs, fabricating surface volume. Holds no inventory.
subclass_oneliner.CROSS_ALPHA_OPERATOR: 🎯 **Behaviour**: cross-Alpha strategy operator — the same signer runs the same playbook across multiple Alpha tokens in parallel, so behaviour is highly correlated. Tracking its entry / exit ≈ tracking a synchronised signal across many tokens.
subclass_oneliner.UNCLASSIFIED_SINGLE_OPERATOR: 🎯 **Behaviour**: single-operator (sub-class TBD) — the on-chain fingerprint is one signer driving all activity, but the counterparty structure or cross-Alpha count doesn't meet any sub-class threshold. Weak signal but worth monitoring.

flow_operators.tail_note: Top {shown} shown above (by this-token tx DESC); the remaining {total} flow operators are all in monitoring/monitoring_paste.json — paste into Binance Wallet / OKX to track.

funding_attribution.title: 💰 High-Value Address Funding Source (mint / DEX / P2P)
funding_attribution.how_to_read: Below are the high-value addresses already surfaced by the wash setup / flow operator / m6 insider / Top-30 holder sections, classified by how they ACQUIRED their tokens over the past 365 days: mint (received directly from 0x0 — covers mining contracts, bridge mint authorities, airdrop mint contracts), DEX buy (received from a known DEX main pool), or P2P (any other EOA transfer, including unidentified CEX withdrawals). Use this to distinguish: ⛏️ high mint% = mining-token operator or sockpuppet airdrop farmer; 🟢 high DEX% = real retail buyer; 🔵 high P2P% = operator aggregation hub or OTC recipient.
funding_attribution.summary_line: Queried {n_queried} high-value addresses; {n_data} have real incoming activity in the past 365 days — ⛏️ Mint-fed {n_mining} / 🟢 DEX-fed {n_dex} / 🔵 P2P-fed {n_p2p}.

rule_11.summary_minted_distributed: Deployer distribution trace: deployer wallet {deployer_short}... minted {mint_amount:,} tokens at {mint_ts}, then pre-launch distributed {distributed_amount:,} to {n_receivers} insider addresses. {n_quiet} quiet wallets (never distributed) collectively hold {quiet_balance:,} tokens.
rule_11.summary_no_pre_launch: Deployer distribution trace: deployer wallet {deployer_short}... minted {mint_amount:,} tokens at {mint_ts}, no pre-launch insider distribution.
rule_11.m6_no_rows: Deployer did not distribute to any wallet within 90 days pre-launch. On-chain only shows mint + LP creation.

monitoring.footer_paste_instruction: Copy the full content of monitoring_paste.json into Binance Wallet or OKX Wallet bulk-import dialog. The platform restores wallet name + emoji tags (🔴 critical / 🟡 watch / 🟠 partially distributed). When these addresses transfer large amounts later, alerts ping for manual review.

llm.lang_directive: Generate ALL narrative content in English. Do not mix languages.

smoke_stub.verdict_one_liner: Token's on-chain fundamentals show 4 quiet wallets still holding 1.3B tokens (= 26% of supply), plus 5 partially-distributed wallets holding 1.4B+ tokens already entering staged distribution. 15+ anomaly transfers in last 24-72h. Recommend holders exit Alpha positions; wait for quiet wallets to fully distribute before re-entry.
smoke_stub.anomaly_verdict_impact: Deployer distribution trace + recent 72h anomaly double signal triggers EXIT_IF_HOLDING. 4 quiet wallets holding 26% of supply is the core future selling pressure source; 100+ recent large transfers indicate accelerating distribution rhythm; verdict baseline downgraded from AVOID 1 tier to EXIT_IF_HOLDING.
smoke_stub.monitoring_footer: Above wallets all under real-time monitoring. Any 1000+ token transfer out, large transfer into CEX hot wallet, or 5+ transactions per hour will trigger an alert via your configured external alerting channel (if any).
smoke_stub.generic: Comprehensive judgment based on deployer distribution trace + recent 72h anomaly detector data.
smoke_stub.interpretation: Data for this section is grounded; key readings and conclusions are consistent. See evidence_graph for corresponding event_id / m6_ref / node_ref.
smoke_stub.nature: Pre-launch distribution from deployer to insider wallet, event recorded.
smoke_stub.detector_detail_rhythm: This wave's distribution rhythm matches historical samples; see evidence_graph.
smoke_stub.detector_detail: Detector category hit, count accumulated; see anomaly.waves above.
smoke_stub.rhythm_title: Pre-launch distribution → post-listing partial dump → recent re-activity
smoke_stub.key_takeaway: Quiet wallets hold 0.5% of supply — primary future selling pressure source.
smoke_stub.m4_note: Before LP creation, deployer distributed to 8 insider addresses: 5 are 100% dumped, 3 remain to observe.
smoke_stub.alert: 1000+ token transfer in/out to CEX triggers alert.
smoke_stub.immediate_action: Sell current holdings in 3 tranches on Alpha within 48h, slippage cap 3%.
smoke_stub.rationale: Trigger price corresponds to the early critical point when quiet wallets begin distributing — prevents cascade dump.
smoke_stub.re_entry: When condition is met, distribution risk has been cleared; can re-evaluate entry.

section_label.fanout_recipient: 🎯 CEX-withdrawal mass distribution / supply control (recipient wallet)

label_name.A1: Quiet accumulation (insider balance concentrated, not distributed)
label_name.B2: Fake depth (LP/mcap mismatch or high vol/LP)
label_name.C1: On-chain confirmed outflow (CEX deposit + DEX swap)

summary.A1: Insider balance concentration {key_holder_pct:.1f}% of total supply, on-chain confirmed outflow < 1% — concentrated chips have not yet left the insider circle
summary.A2_stale: {hubs} hub wallets split chips from CEX sources into downstream sub-wallets, diluting top-holder concentration. ⚠️ The net chip-control magnitude is stale or its SQL was truncated in this run; the old gross figure ({metric_source}) is untrustworthy — re-run the latest pipeline to get the net % figure.
summary.A2_circ: {hubs} hub wallets split chips from CEX sources into {rcpts} sub-wallets (net chip-control **{fout_circ:.2f}% of circulating** / {fout:.2f}% of total supply), diluting top-holder concentration
summary.A2_supply: {hubs} hub wallets split chips from CEX sources into {rcpts} sub-wallets (net chip-control {fout:.2f}% of total supply), diluting top-holder concentration
summary.A3: {mint_count} mint-authority contracts present (cumulative mint = {mint_sum:.1f}% of circulating supply), an ongoing supply source — future mint → dump is possible
summary.B1: {swap:,} on-chain matches in 24h across {sellers} on-chain trading addresses, a single wash bot accounting for {share_pct:.1f}% — the 24h volume contains heavy wash trading and does not equal real absorption
summary.B2_ratio: Volume/liquidity ratio = {vol_lp:.1f}× / liquidity/mcap ratio = {lp_mcap:.4f} — surface liquidity is low and a single trade has large price impact
summary.B2_thin: Liquidity ${lp_usd:,.0f} is thin; a single trade incurs high slippage
summary.C1: On-chain confirmed outflow ${net_sell:,.0f} ({sell_pct:.2f}% of circulating) — real insider realization observed via CEX deposit + on-chain match paths
summary.C2: {ht_count} high-frequency operator wallets (cumulative **throughput** = {ht_pct:.0f}% of total supply; throughput = token in/out flow, NOT sell volume) — already exited historically, balance near 0
summary.C3: {a72} large anomalous transfers in the last 72h (count, not token amount){trunc_note} — on-chain activity is elevated
summary.D1: {cs} wallets have balance hits on both this token and other Alpha tokens (cross-symbol detector) — possibly cross-symbol operators / institutional addresses / VC sub-wallet clusters
summary.D2: This token is deployed on {cg} chains ({non_prim} non-primary chains have independent on-chain hits) — cross-chain dump paths require separate monitoring

mon.reason_mint_authority: Sustainable mint supply source; any transfer-out or new mint changes the forensic read
mon.reason_bridge_contract: Bridge / staking / mining contract; its own on-chain outflow immediately forms a sell-out path
mon.reason_cex_fanout_hub: Relay hub that withdraws from a CEX then splits chips across multiple sub-wallets; the chip-control core
mon.reason_suspected_operator_reserve: Heuristically-flagged hidden operator ammo (large ≥10% circulating + no Arkham label); very likely project reserve / OTC absorption / MM warehouse. Once it starts splitting across addresses / depositing to an exchange / matching on-chain, that is a strong signal the project is starting to dump
mon.reason_fake_mining_cluster_member: Fake-mining mint-cluster member (mint-authority contract → a few large accounts pattern, not real miners). An operator-controlled allocation-recipient cluster; any transfer-out is treated as an operator action
mon.reason_direct_dumper: Insider wallet with on-chain-confirmed outflow observed at a DEX swap or CEX deposit address
mon.reason_deployer: Deployer / insider-distribution wallet; balance changes map directly to project actions
mon.reason_treasury_vesting: vesting / treasury / multisig; unlock or active transfer-out marks known sell-pressure timing
mon.reason_high_throughput_operator: Historically high-frequency liquidating operator; re-receiving tokens = a new distribution round starting
mon.reason_fanout_recipient: Recipient wallet of a large-scale distribution hub; simultaneous transfer to DEX / CEX = sell-out phase starting
mon.reason_anomaly_participant: On-chain participant in a recent-72h anomaly large order; currently active
mon.reason_cross_alpha_active_operator: Operator with high-frequency tx across 5+ Alpha tokens; a cross-token operator
mon.reason_cross_alpha_inactive_whale: Hits balance across multiple tokens but recently inactive; large moves warrant monitoring
mon.reason_cex_deposit_destination: CEX deposit address that multiple insiders transfer into
mon.reason_unknown_top_holder: Unidentified top-balance wallet; balance changes reflect retail vs insider concentration
mon.reason_dex_pool: The DEX main pool itself; large LP moves reflect liquidity events
mon.reason_router_aggregator: DEX router / aggregator; millions of tx per day, monitoring is meaningless
mon.reason_public_cex_hot_wallet: Public CEX hot wallet; its flow noise drowns out the real signal
mon.reason_other: Unclassified wallet; kept for evidence only by default, not actively monitored
mon.trigger_suspected_operator_reserve: Tag type: heuristically-flagged hidden operator ammo (≥ 10% circulating + no Arkham label). The user can watch whether / when these three on-chain behaviours occur: (1) splitting across multiple downstream accounts, (2) transferring into an Arkham-labeled exchange deposit address, (3) on-chain matching (direct transfer-out against USDT/BNB/WBNB etc.). If (2)(3) appear, combine with this report's confirmed sell-out section insider self-sell TWAP to estimate the actual realized USD.
mon.trigger_fake_mining_cluster_member: Tag type: fake-mining mint-cluster member (mint-authority contract → a few large accounts). The user can watch whether / when these three on-chain behaviours occur: (1) transferring into an exchange deposit address after receiving tokens, (2) on-chain matching, (3) splitting across downstream sub-accounts. If (1)(2) appear, combine with this report's confirmed sell-out section to estimate the actual realization of the minted allocation.
mon.trigger_critical: On-chain behaviour reference: balance change / token-in / transfer-out — linked behaviour tags {behaviors}
mon.trigger_high: On-chain behaviour reference: large (≥ $10k) transfer into a DEX router / CEX deposit address
mon.trigger_normal: Listed in bulk for cross-checking, low priority
mon.trigger_not_tracked: Not exported to paste.json (infrastructure / public wallet, flow too high)

render.screen_summary_intro: This section is deterministically derived from on-chain detector outputs and is not buy/sell advice. Full evidence is in the sections below.
render.tldr_summary_intro: This section is for quick retail reading / AI re-interpretation. Detailed forensic detection is in the sections below (with English terms). Everything here is derived from on-chain data and contains no buy/sell advice.
render.tldr_confirmed_realization: **Confirmed insider on-chain realization**: **{tokens} tokens ({pct}% of circulating)** ≈ **${usd} USD** — a lower bound on what insider wallets have realized themselves via (a) deposits to centralized-exchange deposit addresses + (b) their own direct on-chain matches; actual sell-out is most likely higher (see 📊 Confirmed sell-out section)
render.tldr_tge_pct_over100: ({pct}% of nominal total supply — exceeds 100% because this is an inflationary bridge token whose on-chain minted amount is several times the nominal supply)
render.tldr_ht_operators: **Historical high-throughput operators surfaced**: {n} wallets ran cumulative **throughput** (token in/out flow, NOT sell volume, includes wash double-counting) of **{tokens} tokens** over 365 days then zeroed out — already exited
render.tldr_cross_chain: **Cross-chain dump trace**: this token is also deployed on **{chains}**, which have their own mint authorities + operator dump activity (see 🔗 cross-chain section below)
render.tldr_completeness_note: **Report-completeness note**: only on-chain-verifiable wallet → DEX/CEX flows are included. Off-chain behaviour (distribution after a CEX withdrawal / OTC transfers / bridges not in the surf index, etc.) is not on-chain-detectable, so real sell-out may exceed the figures here. See the 🟡 completeness note below.
render.tldr_how_to_use: **How to use this report**: read it alongside the **Monitored wallets** section below; add the core wallets to Binance Wallet / OKX monitoring (monitoring_paste.json supports one-click import). Make entry/exit decisions according to your own risk tolerance.
render.banner_primary_chain_unreliable: ⚠️ **Primary-chain determination unreliable (fetch error)**: the chain(s) where the surf token-holders call failed = `{failed_chain}`. Their LP USD was not retrieved, so the current primary was picked from the remaining chains — **the failed chain's actual LP could be larger**. Re-run the pipeline, or manually confirm via the surf CLI: `surf token-holders --address <CA> --chain <failed_chain> --include labels --json`.
render.banner_realtime_not_ready: ⚠️ **Real-time market data not ready**: neither surf `project-detail` nor the Binance Alpha API returned a live price for this token. The "Token market" table below is skipped. Verify temporarily via BscScan + the main pool address.
render.banner_holder_snapshot_line1: ℹ️ **{chain} on-chain detection coverage note**: surf's `onchain-sql` currently **does not cover** {chain}
render.banner_holder_snapshot_line3: surf only provides on-chain SQL on BSC / Ethereum / Arbitrum / Base / Polygon / Optimism).
render.banner_holder_snapshot_skip_intro: This report **automatically skips** the following forensic sections, which depend on surf SQL transfers tables:
render.banner_holder_snapshot_skip_1: Pre-launch insider distribution trace (Rule 11 deployer trace)
render.banner_holder_snapshot_skip_3: Recent 72h anomaly large transfers (anomaly transfer SQL)
render.banner_holder_snapshot_skip_5: LP 24h net flow + LP creation time (lp_24h_flow / TGE first-trade SQL)
render.banner_holder_snapshot_kept: **Kept**: Alpha API real-time market data (price/vol/mcap/FDV/holder count), surf `token-holders` (top-50 holdings + Arkham labels), section_alloc, monitoring_paste export.
render.banner_holder_snapshot_manual: **Manual verification**: use Solscan / Birdeye / DEX Screener for depth + holder distribution; use the Helius / Shyft APIs to pull SPL Token Program mint / transfer instructions.
render.callout_must_read_title: ⚠️ **Read first — on-chain detection limits / data caveats**
render.callout_wash_dominated: 🚨 **24h volume / on-chain matches dominated by a wash bot**: {n_sellers} counterparty addresses / {total_swaps} on-chain matches, with a single wash bot doing up to {top_swaps} wash matches
render.callout_wash_dominated_tail: **Do not use 24h volume to judge real absorption** — see "absorption cap (5% depth)" below. Detail → 📊 Confirmed sell-out section.
render.callout_mining_model: ⚪ **Mining / bridge-token model**: the deployer only minted a tiny placeholder % + 0 pre-launch outflows, so rule_11's standard m6 trace finds no insiders. dump_tracker's (a) CEX deposit / (b) DEX self-sell queries return 0 on an empty wallet list — **a structural result, not a surf failure**. Real dump data comes from reverse attribution, see → 💰 High-value address funding source section.
render.callout_completeness_low: 🟡 **Several dump paths are structurally out of coverage**: ⛓️ cross-chain dumps (other chains invisible) / 🌉 bridge mint-authority self-dumps (mint source selling itself) / 🏦 the CEX-withdrawal phase (off-chain, not on-chain-detectable) / ⏰ 60d+ historical high-throughput dumps. **Real sell-out may be 5-50× the figures here**. v0.7.24 backlog: mint-authority + multi-chain + high-throughput detectors added (see 🌉 / 🌊 / 🔗 sections at the end).
render.callout_data_incomplete_title: ⚠️ **Data-gap warning — the following sections are affected by surf instability; their numbers are unreliable**:
render.callout_dump_bad: 🟥 **`dump_tracker` section**: at least one of the CEX/DEX confirmation query / insider balance / DEX-sell profile failed after repeated surf retries → the "pure insider holdings / insider-tree holdings / gross sell-out / net sell-out / wash_dominated" fields are **unreliable**; 0 does not mean no insider sell-pressure.
render.callout_wash_bad: 🟥 **`wash_infrastructure` section**: candidate-wallet scanning only processed {processed}/{total} within the wall-clock budget ({budget}s) → "0 wash hits" **does not mean no wash setup**, just that the scan didn't finish.
render.callout_flow_bad: 🟥 **`flow_operators` section**: {n} candidate wallets were prepared but the detection SQL failed entirely on surf (0 credits billed) → "0 operator hits" **does not mean no operators**, the detector didn't actually run.
render.callout_data_incomplete_advice: **Recommendation**: re-run once the surf backend is stable. The current verdict relies mainly on the lineage / anomaly / liq sections (which did run), but on-chain detection completeness is low (~50-60%).
render.chain_state_dormant_label: Dormant insider, undistributed — {n} wallets hold {pct}% of supply; timing is unpredictable
render.chain_state_watch_label: Recent minor activity — {n} transfers but no primary signal triggered
render.chain_state_dumped_label: Historical operator already distributed — {n} insider wallets transferred out ≥95%
render.decision_blindspots: Blind spots you must cross-check yourself
render.decision_5tier_title: 🎯 **Meaning of the 5 on-chain-state labels** (deterministically derived at render time, describing on-chain detection state only, not trading advice):
render.decision_5tier_clean: **No significant trigger** — no significant on-chain detection signal (risk 0-2)
render.decision_5tier_watch: **Watching** — some activity but no primary signal triggered (risk 2-5)
render.decision_5tier_dumped: **Distributed and exited** — historical operator already distributed; on-chain historical sell-pressure released (risk 4-7)
render.decision_5tier_dormant: **Dormant insider, undistributed** — dormant insider wallets have not distributed; timing unpredictable (risk 6-9)
render.decision_5tier_recent: **Recent distribution** — large on-chain activity in the last 72h (risk 7-10)
render.behavior_intro: This section translates 10 detector outputs into 4 categories of "what the operator might be doing." Multi-label by design — one token can hit several categories at once (e.g. A1 accumulation + B1 wash volume + C3 recent activity). Order: severity (🔴 STRONG / 🟠 MEDIUM / 🟡 WEAK) → category → label ID. Describes on-chain facts only, not trading advice.
render.behavior_footer: **Behaviour profile is not the verdict**: the 🎯 on-chain state label above is a composite of 5 tiers (No significant trigger / Watching / Distributed and exited / Dormant insider / Recent distribution); the behaviour profile here is the finer-grained 10-class on-chain detection signals. A token can be "Recent distribution" while also hitting multiple labels like A1+A2+A3+B1+C2+C3.
render.behavior_none: ℹ️ **No major behaviour profile triggered** — all 10 on-chain detection signal detectors returned OFF. This usually happens with: (a) Solana / non-EVM chain aborts (no surf SQL coverage); (b) freshly-deployed unlisted tokens (insufficient data); (c) genuinely clean tokens. Read it together with the 🎯 on-chain state above and the top on-chain-detection-limit callout.
render.anomaly_summary: {n_waves} distribution waves, {n_events} events total (click to expand the full timeline + wallet flows)
render.chip_tail_fanout_warn: ⚠️ of which {n} CEX fan-out recipients are suspected operator wallets
render.chip_op_ammo_unminted: (⚠️ Alpha API reports {alpha_circ}M circulating, on-chain dumpable {chain_circ}M = {ratio}x
render.chip_op_ammo_overest: , Alpha overstates (includes mint authority / vesting lockup)
render.chip_cex_pool: exchange transit pool (retail vs project custody indistinguishable)
render.chip_no_seller_detected: ⚠️ **detector did not identify operator sells** — on-chain net sell = 0 means the detector threshold was not met (mining_fed / mint_authority / m6 insider DEX swap all uncaptured); it does not mean there were no sells — see the funding_attribution + 24h vol sections
render.chip_sanity_title: ⚠️ **Three-bucket sanity check triggered** — the following buckets are ≈ 0%, investigate:
render.chip_sanity_op_ammo: Rare — Alpha-class tokens usually have operator control. Check: (a) is `_master_cluster_addrs` empty (should be ≥ 50)? (b) did the m6 / mint_authority / cex_fanout / ht_dumpers detectors run? (c) inspect the funding_attribution segments
render.chip_sanity_cex_pool: Mainstream BSC Alpha tokens usually reach Binance/Gate/Bitget. Check: (a) is the surf top_holders cex category empty? (b) are the cex_hot_wallet labels complete (Arkham)? (c) the CEX source addresses detected by cex_fanout hubs should be in cex_pool
render.chip_sanity_nonop: Usually surf data is thin — surf BSC top 100 returned {n} wallets (Alpha reports {alpha_holders} holders). About {tail_n} retail holders are in the tail (each < 0.1%, not in the surf top), so the quick-read % is a "within top {n}" ratio, **not a network-wide ratio**. Real retail % > 0
render.chip_pump_explainer: When the operator wants to pump, **the potential sellers = the held chips that don't belong to the operator**. The smaller this is, the more confident the operator is to pump (less fear of being dumped on); the larger, the more cautious.
render.chip_op_interp_high: **High control** — low theoretical pump resistance, but also strong active-distribution capacity
render.chip_retail_interp_light: **Very light external counterparty** — low risk of being dumped on during a pump
render.chip_retail_interp_note: (includes retail whales + protocol contracts + bridge transit)
render.chip_cex_interp: {n} exchange hot/cold/deposit wallets. On-chain it is **impossible to distinguish** retail deposit aggregation vs project custody reserve. During a pump it may flow out from either side
render.chip_judgment_high: **High control + light external counterparty** — the operator has active-distribution capacity and low pump resistance, but the distribution pace determines the one-sided move.
render.chip_judgment_mid: **Project-controlled chips ≥ 50%** — the operator has the conditions to control the book. Read with "insider confirmed realization" below to see whether it is in the active-distribution phase.
render.chip_judgment_low: **Project-controlled chips < 50%** — control is weak; the external counterparty + CEX pool flow direction decide the book.
render.chip_raw_leak_note: **Raw bucket total {raw}% (expand for detail)** — wallet-level overlap across buckets is a debug caveat and does not affect the quick-read {ammo}% operator-ammo conclusion.
render.chip_details_summary: 🔍 Expand: operator-ammo 11-sub-bucket detail (with raw total + overlap note)
render.chip_sb_1a: ①a Public lockup / treasury / airdrop contract outside m6 lineage
render.chip_sb_1a_note: Sablier / Hedgey / custom lockups. Public release schedule, **won't dump immediately during a pump**. Lower bound after subtracting the {reserve} unminted reserve
render.chip_sb_1b: ①b Movable multisig outside m6 lineage
render.chip_sb_1b_note: Gnosis Safe Proxy etc. The operator can transfer today with one signature
render.chip_sb_2: ② m6 lineage portion in circulation
render.chip_sb_2_note: m6 lineage total holdings {tree} minus lockup {locked}. Includes pure insiders ({pure})
render.chip_sb_3_note: Outside m6 lineage; operator routes via exchange → hub wallet → split out
render.chip_sb_3_stale: ⚠️ ③ Exchange-withdrawal distribution (net control not computable)
render.chip_sb_3_stale_note: Gross inflow; phase-2 SQL truncated; net fan-out cannot be computed.
render.chip_sb_4_note: DEX pools are 99% provided by the project / market maker
render.chip_sb_5_note: {n} wallets — flow operators / cross-token whales / high-throughput exit operators
render.chip_sb_6_note: Mining / bridge mint contract current balance; operator-controlled unreleased supply
render.chip_sb_6p: ⑥' Minted to operator cluster (net holdings)
render.chip_sb_6p_note: Cumulative mint - contract reserve - realized = still in the fake-mining cluster
render.chip_sb_6pp_note: {n} wallets. **The 3% threshold risks false positives** — cross-check via monitoring_paste
render.chip_sb_unminted_note: **Not in the circulating denominator — but operator-controlled**. Adds sell-pressure once lockup / mint cadence releases it into circulation
render.chip_sb_raw_total_leak: ⚠️ > 100% = wallet-level overlap across sub-buckets (an in-m6 multisig counted in both ①b + ②). The quick-read {ammo}% is a strictly de-duplicated wallet-level back-calculation
render.chip_sb_raw_total_noleak: Sum of buckets assuming no overlap. Upper-bound estimate
render.chip_explainer_1: The quick-read "operator ammo" is a **wallet-level back-calculation**: iterate the top 100 and de-dup-sum those in the operator set. Different from the raw total — raw is per-bucket upper bound (with overlap), quick-read is strictly de-duplicated.
render.chip_explainer_2: The quick-read "non-operator sell-pressure" includes **retail whales + protocol contracts (Wormhole / veVELVET etc.)**, not all of which is true retail. It is an upper bound.
render.chip_explainer_3: ①a vesting / ⑥ mint reserve → **won't dump immediately** during a pump; enters circulation only over the medium term.
render.chip_explainer_4: ①b multisig / ⑥' cluster → **transferable today**, different in nature from "lockup".
render.chip_explainer_5: Algorithm version: operator-ammo / non-operator-sell-pressure reverse algorithm v0.8.4.8.
render.chip_nonop_detail_h4: 🔍 Verifiable non-operator sell-pressure wallet detail (reverse calc)
render.chip_nonop_detail_intro: Top-100 holders that are **neither in the operator set nor the exchange pool**. Operator set: m6 lineage + multisig / public lockup / treasury / airdrop contract / DEX pool + heuristic catches + detector hits. **All exchange wallets are excluded** (placed in the neutral "exchange transit pool", retail deposits vs project custody indistinguishable). Non-operator sell-pressure mainly comprises true retail whales + bridge protocol contracts + DeFi protocol contracts.
render.chip_nonop_total_label: Total verifiable non-operator sell-pressure (within top 100 holders)
render.chip_nonop_nature_note: ⚠️ **Nature of non-operator sell-pressure**: mainly true retail whales + protocol contracts like Wormhole / veVELVET (user-deposited) + bridge transit. Large bare addresses (no Arkham label + > 1% circulating) are suspect as either operator aliases or true retail whales — cross-check wallet activity via monitoring_paste below.
render.chip_pump_mismatch: 🚨 **This token is 24h +{chg}% yet this algorithm reports {retail}% non-operator sell-pressure** — the numbers clearly mismatch. That the operator dared pump to +{chg}% implies the real counterparty is far below {retail}%. Outside the 5 buckets there are likely unidentified MM / VC reserve / operator-alias wallets — real non-operator sell-pressure may be **< 20-30%**; precise figures need the v0.8.2 enhanced detector. Treat current numbers as reference only — **do not assume {retail}% sell-pressure exists**.
render.lp_source_derived: Main-pool TVL back-derived (= LP USD / 2 / price)
render.dt_no_m6_lineage: mining / bridge token, no pre-launch m6 lineage
render.dt_pure_insider_label: Pure insider current holdings (the true dormant sell-pressure the verdict references)
render.dt_pure_insider_value: {pct}% of supply** ({tokens} tokens, **excluding** vesting / multisig / treasury / CEX custody / DEX routing
render.dt_pure_incl_mfo: ; includes mining-fed operator wallet current balance
render.dt_mfo_dumped_clean: — ⛏️ mining-fed already sold out clean, balance ≈ 0
render.dt_mfo_still_sit: — ⛏️ mining-fed still sits on {tokens} tokens awaiting sell-out
render.dt_mad_dumped_clean: · 🌉 mint authority already sold out clean
render.dt_mad_still_sit: · 🌉 mint authority still sits on {tokens} tokens (can keep mint→dumping at any time)
render.dt_tree_label: Insider-tree current holdings (incl. lockup, conservation anchor)
render.dt_tree_mining: , in mining mode the tree = standard insiders + mining-fed set
render.dt_tree_standard: , includes unreleased lockup + CEX/DEX transit balances, **not equal to the insider-controlled figure**
render.dt_row_a_incl_cex: (includes the mining-fed + mint-authority CEX-deposit portion of {tokens} tokens)
render.dt_row_a_all_dex: (on-chain verified: the mining-fed + mint-authority top destinations are **all** DEX pools / aggregators — the operator chose the DEX path to dump and did not deposit to a CEX on-chain)
render.dt_row_a_caveat: _💡 Note: off-chain CEX withdrawals (CEX → wallet) are not in row (a) — (a) only tracks on-chain wallet → CEX deposits. A project that withdraws from a CEX first and then dumps (e.g. the H case Eleve described) does so off-chain and cannot be on-chain-detected; any subsequent on-chain transfer that dumps via a DEX router is already captured in row (b)._
render.dt_row_b_mfo: ; mining-fed accounts for {tokens} tokens / {swaps} swaps ≈ **${usd} USD**
render.dt_net_dex_cex: ; DEX real ≈ ${dex_usd} (on-chain SUM amount_usd, 100% provable) + CEX estimate ≈ ${cex_usd} (cex_tokens × TWAP)
render.dt_net_above_gross: · ⚠️ Net > Gross +{pct}% (the apparatus traded above the wash price — not a cap breach but high-price realization)
render.dt_net_unreliable: **Cannot reliably estimate** — mining / bridge token with an empty m6 lineage, so dump_tracker does not run the net algorithm. Reference: the 100 ht_dumpers' total **throughput** is {tokens} tokens (= {circ_x} of circulating), which includes **a lot of wash, not all of which is selling**. Real selling < throughput, possibly on the order of 0.1-0.5x circulating, but depends on the operator's degree of wash
render.dt_capped_note: ℹ️ Confirmed sell-out is capped at the **conservation bound** (total supply − current holdings): the gross CEX-deposit flow exceeded the amount physically leaving the insider set (inflated by deposit→withdraw→re-deposit round-trips), so the conservation value is used — i.e. "essentially everything that left the insider tree went into CEX/DEX". Real selling ≈ this, no longer a pure lower bound.
render.dt_window_intro: **📅 Time-window split** — reconcile against short-term events (Twitter sudden-dump alerts / large moves); the relationship is **last 7d ≤ last 30d ≤ cumulative**.
render.dt_window_explainer: Interpretation: **last 7 days** shows current heat (reconcile directly with EmberCN-style sudden alarms); **last 30 days** shows the month's pace; **cumulative** = everything within the surf query window. A single-day burst may not show in the 7d average but is already in the cumulative. CEX route = tokens that went into exchange deposit/hot wallets (sold off-chain on the CEX side, not at the wash price).
render.hoa_observed_prefix: 🕵️ **Hidden operator ammo has shown realization activity**: the tracked **{n} hidden-ammo wallets** (heuristic catches / fake-mining mint cluster) have, via (a) deposits to exchange deposit addresses of **{cex_tokens} tokens** ({n_cex} exchanges: {cex_brands}) + (b) their own on-chain matches of **{dex_tokens} tokens** ({n_swaps} swaps), totalled **{total} tokens**
render.hoa_usd_src_own_dex: (unit price back-derived from the hidden-ammo wallets' own on-chain match USD)
render.hoa_usd_src_own_dex_only: (hidden-ammo's own on-chain match USD, part b only)
render.hoa_usd_src_m6_twap: (rough estimate: using the m6 insider self-sell DEX TWAP; cross-group basis may not align)
render.hoa_observed_tail: **These wallets are outside the algorithm's 5 buckets** and may add on top of "confirmed gross sell-out" to view real distribution; but **you must subtract any wallet overlap with the m6 insider / mining-fed sets** (v0.8.4 backlog: add disjoint dedupe).
render.hoa_none_prefix: 🕵️ Tracking {n} hidden operator-ammo wallets (heuristic / fake-mining mint cluster):
render.hoa_none_tail: no realization observed via (a) deposits to exchange deposit addresses + (b) own on-chain matches
render.hoa_none_accumulating: . The project / market maker may still be in the accumulation phase, distribution not yet started.
render.hoa_err: 🟥 Hidden operator-ammo realization probe SQL failed — `{err}`. Insufficient data — do not conclude "no realization observed"; re-run once surf is stable.
render.dt_mining_model_note_prefix: ⚪ **(a)(b) being 0 is a structural result of the mining / bridge-token model, not a surf failure** — for H-class tokens the deployer is a placeholder, rule_11's standard insider set is empty, and dump_tracker's (a) CEX deposit / (b) DEX self-sell queries return 0 on an empty wallet list. **Real dump data has surfaced in row (c) + the ⛏️ Mining-fed wallet sell-out detail subsection below**
render.dt_incomplete_note: ⚠️ **Confirmed sell-out incomplete**: the CEX/DEX confirmation query still failed after repeated surf retries, so the figures above may understate further. Re-run or verify manually on BscScan.
render.dt_wash_dominated_note: ⚠️ **This token's on-chain dump is dominated by a wash bot**: {n_sellers} counterparty addresses with {total_swaps} on-chain matches total, a single wash bot doing up to {top_swaps} wash matches. Many insiders route tokens to relays / bots before selling (this portion is **unattributable** and not counted in the confirmed lower bound above — so real sell-out is most likely higher than the lower bound). Massive wash = dumping while inflating surface activity to lure buyers.
render.holdings_inflationary_banner: ⚠️ **Alpha API totalSupply disagrees with actual on-chain holdings**: top holdings sum to **{holders_sum}** tokens, exceeding the Alpha-API-reported totalSupply of **{alpha_supply}** ({pct}% > 100%). This usually means the token is **inflationary** (mint emission / staking rewards / DAO issuance) and the on-chain totalSupply already exceeds the Alpha API snapshot. The "% of supply" in the table below is computed from static Alpha data, so the real share is lower. Verify the latest `totalSupply()` via a chain explorer (etherscan / basescan).
render.holdings_takeaway_m6: Insider tree: **{total}** wallets hold **{tree_pct}%** of supply cumulatively, of which {full} fully distributed (each ≈ 0) · {partial} distributing · {quiet} near-zero holdings
render.holdings_takeaway_mining: Insider tracking: **{n}** wallets hold **{tree_pct}%** of supply cumulatively
render.holdings_takeaway_mining_tail: (mining / bridge token, no pre-launch m6 lineage).
render.holdings_takeaway_outflow: Insiders have on-chain-confirmed outflow of **{usd}M USD** = **{pct}%** of circulating, priced at the insider self-sell TWAP (not the wash quote).
render.holdings_takeaway_wash: A single wash bot accounts for **{pct}%** of 24h on-chain matches — surface volume is unreliable; judge real absorption by 5% depth.
render.lineage_summary: {n} insider wallets: {full} fully distributed / {partial} distributing / {quiet} quiet (click to expand the full lineage + wallet balances + distribution rate)
render.lineage_stats: Stats (full m6 lineage, **{total}** wallets): {quiet} near-zero holdings / public-lockup custody · {partial} distributing · {full} fully distributed
render.step4_gap_intro: **⚠️ Step4 data gap**: {n} dumpers were skipped after surf chunk failures (any single chunk error → the whole dumper is dropped to prevent undercount-driven false upgrades). Affected addresses + errored-chunk ratio:
render.step4_gap_action: Action: re-run (a transient surf throttle usually self-heals) — or re-run with lower concurrency via `BINANCE_ALPHA_STEP4_WORKERS=3`.
render.step4_cap_intro: **⚠️ Step4 budget-cap truncation (data gap — conclusion is a lower bound)**: the dispersal tree is wider than the step4 recursion budget (default 12); the largest sub-dumpers were expanded and **{n} additional sub-dumpers (~{tokens} tokens cumulative) were not recursively expanded**. The current verdict is based on the expanded portion and is a **lower bound** — the real operator network may be deeper.
render.step4_cap_action: Action: this is a budget truncation, not a surf failure. For full depth, re-run with `BINANCE_ALPHA_STEP4_MAX_DUMPERS=40`.
render.fa_error: 🟥 **Funding-source section surf failure** — `{err}`. The high-value addresses ({n}) are unclassified by source; the wallets flagged in the wash / flow / dump sections above are still valid but lack the mint-vs-DEX-buy distinction. Re-run once surf is stable.
render.fa_mining_dominant: ⚠️ **{n_mining}/{n_data} high-value addresses ({pct}%) got their tokens mainly from mint (mining / bridge / airdrop)** — not DEX buying. Such wallets are ① operators of mining / bridge tokens ② sockpuppet airdrop-farming clusters ③ project wallets from vesting unlocks. Their subsequent DEX dumps / CEX deposits are dumping, not retail PnL-taking.
render.fa_scan_cap: Scan cap: the pipeline collected {total} high-value candidates, max_addrs cap = 200, actually queried {queried} (ordered by detector priority: wash → flow → m6 → dump-sellers → Top-30 holders). Truncated {truncated} — common on PLAY-class tokens with many (60+) flow_operators.
render.fa_cex_withdraw_note: The CEX-withdrawal column is not yet separately identified (a v0.7.24 candidate) and is currently grouped under P2P. When a real CEX hot-wallet transfer cannot be distinguished from a plain EOA transfer, both count as P2P.
render.mfo_error: 🟥 **Mining-fed sell-out query surf failure** — `{err}`. The DEX-dump data for the ⛏️ mint-dominant addresses in the table above was not retrieved this run.
render.mfo_intro: Mining-fed wallets ({n}) sold via DEX over 365 days: **{sold_tokens} tokens ≈ ${sold_usd} USD**, total on-chain outflow {outflow} tokens. **This is the real dump data dump_tracker cannot reach under the mining / bridge-token model** — the path where the operator claims tokens from the mining contract then realizes them.
render.mauth_intro: Detected **{n} mint-authority contracts** (receive mint from 0x0, excluding the deployer + wallets already covered in the mining-fed section). They are bridge / staking / airdrop contracts that may **themselves** DEX swap. This is a dump path the v0.7.23.x series missed entirely.
render.mauth_inflationary: ⚠️ **On-chain total mint {minted} already exceeds nominal supply ({supply}) — this is an inflationary bridge token with a non-stop mint→dump→burn cycle. The "total supply 10B" figure is reference-only; the real on-chain circulating amount is paced by the mint authorities.**
render.mauth_selfdex_summary: 🌉 The mint authorities **themselves** sold via DEX over 365d: **{tokens} tokens ≈ ${usd} USD**. This is the portion the bridge contract swapped out directly — a path independent of the mining-fed operators' dumping; **real total sell-out should be the sum of both**.
render.htd_intro: Detected **{n} operator wallets** with a high-throughput clear-out pattern (large token flow-through + balance ≈ 0 + high-frequency tx). Thresholds: throughput 1M ~ 5% of supply, balance < 5% of throughput, n_tx ≥ 1000. Already filtered out infra labels like DEX routers / CEX deposits / aggregators (not operators). These are operators that finished dumping and left before the 60d window — missed by flow_operators (60d window) + mining-fed (balance > threshold).
render.htd_dedup_note: 💡 v0.7.28 selective dedupe: the {n} rows marked "see primary section" are wallets that also hit other sections, with full detail expanded there. Balance / 365d inflow are still shown here for scanning; jump to the primary section for Arkham labels. Full dump in skeleton.json `address_role_index` top.
render.htd_top20_note: Showing top 20; {n} high-throughput operator wallets detected in total (sorted by throughput; throughput = token in/out flow, NOT sell volume). Full list in skeleton.json `funding_attribution.high_throughput_dumpers.dumpers`.
render.htd_footer: 🌊 These wallets passed **{throughput} tokens** through over 365d then cleared out — operators that have finished dumping. They've already sold, but **if the project mints another batch and distributes it to similar-pattern wallets in future**, they become the next dump-risk source.
render.cfh_intro: Detected **{n} hub wallets** whose largest funding source is an Arkham-labeled centralized-exchange deposit / hot-wallet address, then distributing to 5-50 new addresses (each at least 100K tokens). This is the typical control pattern of "operator withdraws from a CEX → splits into retail-looking holdings" — used to evade top-holder concentration detection.
render.cfh_threshold: ⚙️ **Detection threshold**: the hub distributes to 5-50 unique addresses (each ≥ 100K tokens) within 365d, and the hub's largest inflow source is Arkham-confirmed as `CEX_DEPOSIT` or `CEX_HOT_WALLET`. Across {n_candidate} candidate hubs, {n_confirmed} passed CEX-label verification.
render.cfh_net_total_tail: — {n} independent wallets (de-duplicated across hubs). This is a **lower bound on real control** via the operator's CEX → hub → split path.
render.cfh_field_recipients_val: **{n} candidates** (Phase 1 count, incl. CEX loopback + inter-hub transfers + retail)
render.cfh_field_net: 🎯 Net control (truly dispersed to independent wallets)
render.cfh_net_recipients: — {n} independent wallets (lower bound on real control after excluding CEX loopback + inter-hub transfers)
render.cfh_field_loopback_val: {tokens} tokens (transferred from the hub back to that hub's CEX deposit address — not control, just a fund-path loop)
render.cfh_field_shuffle_val: {tokens} tokens (transfers between hubs — multi-hub coordination by the same operator)
render.cfh_phase1_deprecated_note: (a v0.7.24e SQL-layer bug, 3-13x higher than the real net; kept for debug reconciliation only — use the 🎯 net-control row above for actual control)
render.cfh_phase1_broken_val: (avg/recipient ≈ {avg}, min {min}) — ⚠️ this is the v0.7.24e Phase 1 SQL output, measured to be 3-13x higher than the Phase 2 detail. The net figure only exists after running v0.8.1 pipeline.
render.cfh_retail_note: 💡 Retail read: these sub-wallets are most likely sockpuppets controlled by the same operator, used to fake a "non-concentrated holdings" picture. Their subsequent DEX dumps / CEX deposits are synchronized — add them to your monitoring list.
render.mc_intro: Detected that this token is deployed on multiple chains. The primary chain's ({chain}) dump activity already surfaced in earlier sections; here we show **other chains'** dump activity. The earlier v0.7.23.x single-chain forensic missed cross-chain entirely.
render.mc_ht_h: {n} high-throughput operators (cleared out after cumulative **throughput** of {throughput} tokens; throughput = token in/out flow, NOT sell volume):
render.mc_no_detection: **No** mint authorities or high-throughput operators detected on this chain (within our thresholds). Possible reasons: (1) the token has little activity on this chain (2) thresholds too strict (3) the operator used P2P rather than high-frequency DEX swaps.
render.mc_footer: 💡 This section only surfaces what the current detectors could fetch cross-chain. For the real cross-chain dump magnitude, combine with on-chain explorers like Etherscan / Bscscan / Arbiscan.
render.wcg_intro: **Wallet ↔ wallet direct-transfer graph clusters** — operator clusters that bypass CEX, mint, and the m6 lineage. Bubblemaps-style algorithm: high-value transfers between candidate wallets (≥0.5% supply per edge) form connected components ≥ 3 nodes. Uses undirected 2-core pruning to exclude single-edge false positives.
render.mon_priority_line1: 📊 **Monitoring priority (v0.7.27 deterministic ranker)**: 🚨 {critical} CRITICAL · 🔥 {high} HIGH · 👀 {normal} NORMAL
render.mon_priority_line2: Wallets in paste.json are sorted by level, 🚨 first. Prioritize CRITICAL+HIGH ({critical_high}) — when these wallets move, the on-chain detection picture changes. NORMAL ({normal}) is for bulk cross-checking, no push notification needed. 💤 NOT_TRACKED are DEX routers / public CEX hot wallets whose flow noise drowns the real signal, removed from paste.
render.mon_top10_note: (the report shows only the top 10; for all {n} wallets use `monitoring/monitoring_paste.json` to one-click paste into Binance Wallet / OKX monitoring)
render.mra_intro: The table below lists wallets that appear in ≥2 detector sections. Retail readers seeing "the same address N times" won't mistake it for N different wallets. AI re-interpretation can get cross-role context without following anchors.
render.mra_footer: {n} multi-role addresses total; the table shows the top 30 by number of matched roles. Full index in the `address_role_index` field of the machine-readable JSON below.
render.abort_invalid_ca_body:
The contract address you entered is not in EVM 0x40-hex format. This skill only runs on-chain detection for EVM chains (BSC / Ethereum / Base / Arbitrum / Polygon / Optimism); Solana base58 addresses / other-chain formats are rejected at the Section A entry, wasting 0 surf credits.

**What to do**:

- If this is a Solana token (pump.fun-class like Fartcoin / Jellyjelly), wait for a future framework version to add Solana support (base58 CA validation + a Solana-specific deployer-trace helper). Track the v0.7.22+ release notes.
- If you pasted the wrong thing, the same project may have an EVM-chain wrapper / bridge — find the corresponding EVM CA in the Binance Alpha list and re-run.
- If it is an EVM CA but malformed (dropped 0x or extra spaces on copy), validate 0x + 40 hex chars and re-run.
render.abort_spot_graduated_body:
This token appears in both the Alpha list AND Binance Spot — it's in the Alpha → Spot graduation transition (mutual-exclusion lag). By design Alpha on-chain detection does not apply — the Spot tier has its own liquidity / regulatory / cross-listing mechanics, and the Alpha-only deployer-trace / wash-detection assumptions no longer hold.

**What to do**: wait for the Alpha list to remove it (usually 1-3 days), after which the CA should be handled on the Spot side only. Don't make decisions off the Alpha on-chain-detection verdict right now.
render.abort_never_alpha_title: Address is not in the Binance Alpha list
render.abort_never_alpha_body:
The Alpha API did not find this CA. Possible reasons:

- It genuinely never listed on Alpha (just some on-chain token)
- It was delisted / retired
- Its chainId is outside Alpha's coverage

**What to do**: check the Binance Alpha site to confirm whether the CA is still listed; if not, Alpha on-chain detection does not apply — use general on-chain analysis tools (Etherscan / Arkham).
render.abort_unsupported_chain_title: The chain reported by the Alpha API is not currently supported
render.abort_unsupported_chain_body:
The Alpha API returned a chainId, but the current chain_router has no mapping for it. Since v0.7.20 the chain_router supports Ethereum / BSC / Base / Arbitrum / Polygon / Optimism EVM chains. Other new chains (e.g. Linea / Scroll / Mantle / Sonic) first need a chain_router mapping + a check that the surf SQL tables exist.

**What to do**: report it to the framework maintainer to add the chain mapping; or manually verify via a chain-specific explorer + Arkham first.
render.abort_missing_chain_id_title: The Alpha API did not return a chainId
render.abort_missing_chain_id_body:
Section A successfully retrieved the Alpha listing info but the entry is missing the chainId field. This is an Alpha API contract violation, and the framework fails loud rather than continue — without chainId there's no way to decide which chain to route SQL to, and a silent fallback would reproduce the v0.7.19.x PLAY routing bug.

**What to do**: retry; if it persists, the upstream Alpha API may be down.
render.abort_unknown_body: Unrecognized abort reason. See the `_detail` field.
render.abort_intro: The pipeline aborted during Section A, **without running any surf SQL** (0 credits consumed).
render.abort_footer: v0.7.21.6: even on abort this short report is generated; earlier versions only printed a stdout note the user couldn't see.
render.tldr_more_behaviors: ({n} more lower-priority behaviour labels hit; see the 🧠 section of the default report for the full list)
render.tldr_behavior_none: ℹ️ **No major behaviour profile triggered** — all 10 on-chain detection signal detectors are OFF. (Solana abort / clean token / insufficient data)
render.tldr_mon_paste: paste.json is sorted by level — import CRITICAL + HIGH first.
render.tldr_mon_nodata: (monitoring-level data not generated — run the default report for the full list)
render.tldr_more_critical: ({n} more CRITICAL wallets; full paste in monitoring_paste.json)
render.tldr_no_critical: (no CRITICAL-level monitored wallets found. Run the default report for HIGH+NORMAL levels.)
render.tldr_caveat_wash: 24H vol dominated by wash (cannot judge real absorption)
render.tldr_caveat_mining: Mining / bridge-token model (empty standard insider set is a structural result)
render.tldr_caveat_cross_chain: Cross-chain ({chains}) has independent on-chain detection hits; see the 🔗 section of the default report
render.tldr_caveat_none: Current on-chain detection completeness did not trigger any major blind-spot callout.
render.tldr_blindspot_footer: The CEX-withdrawal phase (off-chain) + OTC + bridges not in the surf index are **structurally invisible**. **Real sell-out may be 5-50× the figures here** — see the top callout of the default report for the full caveat.
render.tldr_mode_footer: TLDR mode. Full on-chain detection detail → run `--mode=default` for the 18-28KB briefing. AI deep-dive → `--mode=deep` (fully expanded). Wallet monitoring import → `monitoring_paste.json`.
render.tldr_disclaimer: Data research only; not a trading instruction or financial opinion.

screen.phase_label_distributing: 🔴 Dumping in progress / selling into the pump
screen.phase_label_recent_unconfirmed: 🟠 Recent anomalies — no large realization confirmed yet
screen.phase_label_dumped: 🟢 Dumped & exited — past operators have left
screen.phase_label_dormant: 🟠 Quiet, not yet distributed — risk unreleased
screen.phase_label_watch: 🟡 Watch — some anomalies below main-signal threshold
screen.phase_ev_dumped: m6 lineage fully distributed; no active insiders
screen.phase_ev_dormant: Quiet insiders hold concentrated positions; timing unpredictable
screen.phase_ev_clean: None of the 5 main signal tiers triggered
screen.chip_ev: Operator/project-controlled chips {op_pct:.1f}% / CEX relay pool {cex_pct:.1f}% / verifiable non-operator sell-pressure {retail_pct:.1f}%
screen.insider_ev: Confirmed insider realization {sell_pct:.1f}% of circ + net CEX-withdrawal distribution {fanout_pct:.1f}% of circ
screen.volume_label_partial_wash: 🟠 Partial wash — discount the real absorption
screen.volume_ev_no_data: Confirmed sell-out section captured no on-chain matches
screen.supply_label_ht_shown: 🟠 High-frequency liquidation visible — but no new mint source
screen.supply_ev_mint_high: {n_mint_auth} mint-authority contracts; cumulative mint = {mint_pct_supply:.1f}% of total supply
screen.supply_ev_mint_limited: {n_mint_auth} mint authorities; cumulative {mint_pct_supply:.1f}% of total supply
screen.supply_ev_ht_shown: {n_ht} high-frequency liquidation wallets; cumulative throughput {ht_throughput_pct:.0f}% of circ
screen.supply_ev_none: No mint authority / low high-frequency liquidation throughput
screen.monitor_item_new_cycle: Watch for a new distribution cycle (new mint / new cluster)
screen.monitor_item_default: No detector triggered a main signal; maintain baseline monitoring

one_sentence_phase.DISTRIBUTING: Dumping in progress / selling into the pump

cross_sym.behavior_base_full_mm: Market-making (MM) framework (24KB+ large contract, Wintermute/Jump/Cumberland style)
cross_sym.behavior_base_lite_mm: Lightweight MM contract (5-24KB, simplified market-making bot)
cross_sym.flow_balanced: in/out roughly balanced (possible MM market-making state)

scope.data_freshness_warning: Underlying data is available up to {latest_date} UTC (on-chain activity in the {lag_hours:.1f}h after that must be checked directly on BscScan)

lineage.label_hint_operator_relay: Operator relay (holding {pct:.2f}% of supply), reached via multi-hop distribution

alloc.label_lockup: Project / infra / distribution pool addresses: {n} (vesting / multisig / treasury / DEX infra / CEX custody / 3rd-party distribution platform / retail claim pool, Arkham-verified)

sec2.liq_note_lp_flow_no_sql: surf does not cover this chain (no SQL table), see Solana banner at top
sec2.update_auto_success: ✅ Auto-update applied ({old} → {new}). Please re-run your last command.
sec2.update_reason_timeout_180s: (timed out at 180s — likely slow network/proxy, retry usually works)
sec2.update_reason_npx_missing: (no Node.js installed locally — install from https://nodejs.org/)
sec2.update_reason_no_change_on_disk: (npx exited 0 but this checkout didn't change — likely manual git clone, run `git pull`)
